MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO VACATE ORDER REQUIRING JUDICIAL ARBITRATION / NOTICE OF PAYMENT OF COURT REPORTER'S FEE (TRANSACTION ID # 63100078) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRIEGO, JERRY

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JERRY GRIEGO'S MOTION TO VACATE ORDER REQUIRING JUDICIAL ARBITRATION AND TO SET CASE FOR TRIAL SETTING IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW WHY HE DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S AUGUST 8, 2018 ORDER AND JOINT STIPULATION, WHICH EXTENDED THE ARBITRATOR'S JURISDICTION THROUGH DECEMBER 31, 2018. EVEN ASSUMING THE COURT HAS THE AUTHORITY TO VACATE ITS PRIOR ORDERS, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN GOOD CAUSE TO DO SO. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PRESENTED ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT A CONCLUSION THAT THE AMOUNT IN CONTROVERSY WILL EXCEED $50,000. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF DID NOT SUPPLEMENT HIS DISCOVERY RESPONSES TO REFER TO HIS ALLEGED ADDITIONAL INJURIES AND DAMAGES. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS APPROVED AS TO FORM AND COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312 REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND EMAIL IT TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: W. TRUPEK, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600 EMAIL MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Order Requiring Judicial Arbitration And To Set Case For Trial Setting
Off Calendar Apr-19-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jessica Dewitt Esq., , Brady Law Group, 800-881-9011, appearing for Plaintiff;
Benjamin Angulo, Esq., Tyson & Mendes, 628-253-5072, appearing for Defendant Mark Whiting, et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court modifies the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JERRY GRIEGO's Motion To Vacate Order Requiring Judicial Arbitration And To Set Case For Trial Setting

Plaintiff Jerry Griego's motion to vacate order requiring judicial arbitration and to set case for trial setting is denied without prejudice. Plaintiff has failed to show why he did not comply with the Court's August 8, 2018 order and joint stipulation, which extended the arbitrator's jurisdiction through December 31, 2018. Even assuming the Court has the authority to vacate its prior orders, Plaintiff has not shown good cause to do so. Plaintiff has not presented admissible evidence sufficient to support a conclusion that the amount in controversy will exceed $50,000. Further, Plaintiff did not supplement his discovery responses to refer to his alleged additional injuries and damages.

Counsel for defendant is to prepare a proposed order that is approved as to form and complies with CRC 3.1312 repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600 email maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
